calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jason Wittig, Plaintiff/Respondent in pro per; Anna Mindess, Certified ASL Interpreter Badge# 400007, assisting Mr. Wittig; George Morris, Esq., counsel for Defendant/Appellant Equifax Credit Information Services, Inc.; Margaret Leslie, Corporate representative for Defendant/Respondent Equifax Credit Information Services, Inc.

Parties, counsel and the interpreter state their appearances. Mr. Morris provides a brief statement of the case and Equifax's position. Mr. Wittig and Ms. Leslie are placed under oath. Mr. Wittig provides a statement of the case and the following exhibits [none of which are marked for identification]:

Equifax Credit Report dated 05/12/11
Call Log [undated] reflecting calls from November 2017 to February 2018
Credit Karma screenshot [undated]
ATT letter re: Equifax Data Breach

Mr. Morris cross-examines Mr. Wittig. Mr. Morris moves for judgment on the case which is denied. Mr. Morris examines Ms. Leslie. Mr. Wittig briefly cross-examines Ms. Leslie.

The parties conclude testimony and the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. D306
